name: Cossack Hetmanate (1649–1764)

colors.symbolism: the historic banner colour of the Zaporozhian Cossack regiments
the figure of the Cossack with a musket

description: A red banner charged with a white Cossack figure shouldering a musket — a reconstructed depiction of the regimental banner of the Zaporozhian Host during the Cossack Hetmanate.
history: The Cossack Hetmanate was a semi-autonomous Cossack polity established by Bohdan Khmelnytsky's 1648 uprising and the 1649 Treaty of Zboriv; it persisted under Russian suzerainty until its abolition by Catherine II in 1764. The figure of the Cossack with a musket (the 'Cossack with a musket') was the regimental seal of the Zaporozhian Host and later the official emblem of the Hetmanate; it remains today the historic basis for the coats of arms of several central-Ukrainian oblasts.

features.role: Cossack with a musket — historic seal of the Zaporozhian Host
features.symbolism: the Cossack with a musket, the official emblem of the Hetmanate
